response.justification: Candidate has strong full-stack and systems experience (TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, some Java/C++), but the role demands deep JVM expertise, Kotlin proficiency, Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate experience, and genuine interest in IDE internals — none of which are evidenced in the resume. Java appears only as a listed skill with limited project context (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tooling years ago), and there is no Kotlin, Spring, or modern enterprise JVM work. The closest IDE-adjacent experience (NI LabWindows/CVI in C/C++, 2014-2015) is brief and not JVM-related.
strengths: Long track record on complex systems and large-scale codebases across multiple languages
Prior IDE-component experience at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) shows tooling exposure
Strong software craftsmanship background — TDD, CI/CD, workshops, open-source maintenance
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin experience and only shallow, dated Java work
No Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience listed anywhere
Recent focus is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not JVM tooling or IDE internals
